title: P&L report in monday vs excel
excerpt: Have any of you done a P&L report on Monday? I am trying to evaluate if it is possible to bring on Monday that report that is currently in Excel with all the formulas and dashboards that it entails.

posts.author: valedemayo (Valentina De Mayo)
posts.cookedHtml: Have any of you done a P&L report on Monday? I am trying to evaluate if it is possible to bring on Monday that report that is currently in Excel with all the formulas and dashboards that it entails.

posts.author: JasperDemeurie (Jasper)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there

It all depends a bit on the vastness of your P&L I guess.
The amount of data entry points.
The amount of calculations in the back.

I’m an e-commerce manager, and basically, it’s too difficult for me to do it in Monday, unless I do a lot of background calculations in the background and than manually put in (or upload) some of the data into Monday.

Either way, once you made your P&L in Excel, having that data in Monday could help you vastly to get a better grasp on it by dashboarding all around it to have visualisations on your P&L though.
